prompt: I'm working in an ABP 10.3 Layered modular monolith. My `Acme.Reports` module needs to read 'how many submissions a given owner has' from the `Acme.Forms` module to build a report. The Forms module already has a `FormSubmissionAppService` with this query. Can I just inject `IFormSubmissionAppService` into my `ReportAppService`? What's the right pattern?
assertions: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'

Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) — NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
